stemHtml: A CAT operator holds an approval to carry out certain activities, but is currently unable to cope with the amount of work to be undertaken. The operator therefore decides to contract out some of the work to another operator, with the same level of approval. Which of the following statements is true?

options.a: Irrespective of the level of approval held by the subcontractor, the work shall be carried out under the approval of the operator contracting work.
options.b: The operator’s compliance monitoring should check that the subcontractor’s approval effectively covers the contracted activities and that it is still valid.
options.c: The type of subcontracting arrangement described is prohibited by the applicable regulations.
options.d: There is NO need to check on the subcontractor’s approval, authorisation procedures.
